description: Add a 'Reset items' action (Guest List only, owner-only) that resets all item rsvp_status to 'not_invited'. Ships on React web and Swift iOS with confirmation dialog, undo support, realtime sync. Also fixes a parity gap in the existing duplicate-list flow (web drops rsvp_status, iOS preserves it) and adds an opt-in 'Reset RSVP statuses' toggle to the duplicate dialog on both platforms.

userStories.title: "Reset items" action in 3-dot menu (Guest List, owner-only)
userStories.description: As a Guest List owner, I want a 'Reset items' option in the list's 3-dot menu so I can clear all RSVP statuses at once.
acceptanceCriteria: List 3-dot menu shows 'Reset items' entry only when list.type === 'guest_list' AND current user is the list owner
Hidden for all other list types
Hidden for shared collaborators (non-owners) even if they have edit rights
Available on both desktop web and mobile web menus
Available on Swift iOS context menu and 3-dot menu
Disabled (or shows 'No items to reset' toast) when the list is empty
Lint passes
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
userStories.notes: Menu entries added on web (4 locations) and iOS (2 locations); gated by isOwned && type==guest_list; disabled when empty. Stub callbacks ready for US-002.

userStories.title: Confirmation dialog before reset
userStories.description: As a user, I want to confirm before resetting so I don't lose RSVP progress accidentally.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping 'Reset items' opens a confirmation dialog
Dialog text: 'Reset all {N} guests to Not Yet Invited? This will clear their current RSVP status.'
Buttons: 'Cancel' (secondary) and 'Reset' (destructive style — red on web, .destructive role on iOS)
If all items are already 'not_invited', skip the dialog and show toast 'All guests are already at Not Yet Invited'
Tapping outside the dialog cancels on web
Cancel preserves all current statuses with no DB write
Lint passes
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
userStories.notes: Web: handleResetItems shows destructive/info ConfirmDialog; ConfirmDialog extended with title + optional cancel + neutral confirm + alertdialog role + aria-labelledby/describedby. iOS: .alert modifiers on both ListDetailView (destructive + info) and ListBrowserView (destructive only — row items not loaded).

userStories.title: Reset operation persists to Supabase (atomic, owner-only)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single batched update so the reset is atomic and fast, and RLS must restrict it to the list owner.
acceptanceCriteria: React: resetGuestListRsvp(listId) in services/database.js runs a single UPDATE items SET rsvp_status = 'not_invited' WHERE list_id = $1
Swift: equivalent ItemService.resetRsvpStatuses(listId:) performs the same single update
One DB round-trip regardless of item count
Updates updated_at on each affected row so realtime fires for collaborators
Operation is idempotent (re-running has no further effect)
RLS permits only the list owner to perform the reset; collaborators are denied
Returns the count of items reset
Lint passes

userStories.title: Realtime sync to collaborators
userStories.description: As a collaborator on a shared guest list, I want to see the reset reflected immediately on my device.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing realtime channel on items table propagates the bulk update
React ShoppingListContext reflects new rsvpStatus: 'not_invited' on all items without page reload
Swift ListDetailViewModel items array updates without manual refresh
RSVP summary counters at the top of the list update live
No duplicate channel subscriptions fire
Lint passes
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Undo support after reset
userStories.description: As a user, I want to undo a reset I didn't mean to perform.
acceptanceCriteria: Web: reset action pushes a single entry onto the undo stack (useUndoStack) capturing previous rsvp_status for each item
Web: undoing replays the previous statuses via batched UPDATE
iOS: reset registers with UndoManager so shake-to-undo restores prior statuses
Toast 'Reset N guests' with an 'Undo' button appears for 5 seconds on web
Undo entry expires per existing undo stack policy
Undo gesture works offline on a best-effort basis if the user has not yet synced
Lint passes
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode

userStories.title: Duplicate List integration — parity fix + opt-in RSVP reset (web + iOS)
userStories.description: As a user duplicating a Guest List, I want a one-click way to also reset RSVP statuses in the new copy, with consistent behavior across web and iOS. prd-duplicate-list shipped on both platforms (despite its doc claiming iOS-only). iOS preserves rsvp_status on duplicate; web drops it because src/services/database.js does not include rsvp_status in the insert. This story fixes that parity gap and then adds an opt-in reset toggle on both platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: Web src/services/database.js duplicateList is updated to copy rsvp_status from source items (match iOS behavior)
Web duplicateList also copies recipe_id and recipe_name if present; audit for any other fields missing vs iOS while making this change
Duplicate dialog/sheet includes a 'Reset RSVP statuses' toggle on both web and iOS
Toggle is only visible when source list is Guest List (list.type === 'guest_list' on web, list.type == 'guest_list' on iOS)
Toggle is defaulted to ON for Guest List duplicates
When ON: the duplicate insert writes rsvp_status = 'not_invited' on all copied items in the initial insert (no insert-then-update)
When OFF: copied items preserve the source rsvp_status (post-parity-fix behavior)
Setting is per-duplicate (not persisted across invocations)
Non-Guest duplicates are unaffected on both platforms — toggle hidden, behavior unchanged
Web and iOS share identical toggle semantics, default state, and user-facing copy

userStories.title: Empty state and edge cases
userStories.description: As a user, I want sensible behavior when reset is run in unusual states.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty list: 'Reset items' is hidden or disabled with an explanatory label or tooltip
All items already 'not_invited': skip the dialog and show a toast 'Already reset'
Items added during an in-flight reset get the default 'invited' status (existing behavior preserved); reset only affects items present at the moment of the action
Network failure mid-reset: show error toast 'Couldn't reset — try again'; no partial writes (transactional or fail-fast)
If the list type changes away from Guest List, the menu entry disappears on next render
Lint passes
Verify in browser
